hook.text: A 'recipe' has a title, ingredients, steps, prep time, and an image. That structure is a content model.

buildup.text: Content models define the structure of each content type: what fields it has, what's required, and how types relate.

discovery.text: Without content models, every page is an HTML blob. With them, content is reusable, searchable, and portable.

twist.text: Structured recipe ingredients power shopping lists, nutrition tools, and voice assistants — not just a web page.

climax.text: Content models separate content from presentation. The same data feeds your website, app, API, and future channels.

punchline.text: Structure once, display everywhere. That's what content models enable.

quiz.question: Why are content models important for multi-channel publishing?
options: Structured content can be reused across websites, apps, APIs, and future channels
They make pages load faster on mobile
Content models are required by search engines for ranking
